conversation.content: You are a customer service agent for an e-commerce company. Today's date: <REDACTED>. You are currently helping customer cust_003. Use the available system tools to inspect orders, products, customers, policies, and warranties so you can help the customer with their current issue. Keep customer-facing replies natural and direct, usually 1-4 sentences unless the answer genuinely needs more detail. Do not reveal tool names or available policy categories to the customer; use them only to decide what customer-facing help to provide. You may say you checked the policy when you did.
I received the shirt from ORD-7215 as a gift. I only need a larger size; please don't refund the person who bought it.

You can absolutely size up without refunding the gift giver.

Because our exchange policy doesn’t allow a “self-swap” for the same item, the way to do this as a gift is:
- We process a return on the original shirt, but issue the value as **store credit to your account**, not back to the original payment.
- You then use that store credit to order the same “Premium Cotton Shirt” in the larger size.

If that works for you, tell me:
1) What size you received, and  
2) What size you need now,  

and I’ll start the return-for-store-credit on ITEM-10215 and help you place the replacement.

rules.must_be_different_product: Exchanges must specify a different product than the original item. Self-swap (exchanging an item for the same product) is not allowed — use process_return for a refund instead.
rules.same_price: Exchange for a same-price product: no charge, no refund.
rules.more_expensive: Exchange for more expensive item: customer pays price difference.
rules.cheaper: Exchange for cheaper item: difference refunded as store credit (not original payment).
rules.out_of_stock: If requested item is out of stock: issue store credit for the original item; do not complete the exchange.
rules.return_window: Must be within return window (same rules as returns).
rules.no_price_protection: No price protection on exchanges (item price at time of purchase applies).

conversation.content:
I really don’t want a return or any kind of store credit—I only want this shirt exchanged directly for the Large size, with no refund or credit to anyone.  

I currently have the Premium Cotton Shirt in Medium and I need the Premium Cotton Shirt in Large instead.

Under our current policy, I’m not allowed to process a direct “same item, different size” exchange in the system—only returns or exchanges to a different product. That’s why I suggested the store-credit route: it’s the only supported way to move you to a different size without sending money back to the buyer.

However, I can still make this as close as possible to a simple size swap:
- We process the original shirt as a **return**, but direct the value **only to you** (store credit on your account), not to the gift giver.
- I then help you immediately use that credit to order the **Premium Cotton Shirt, Large**, so it functions like a straight exchange in practice.

If you’re willing to proceed that way, I’ll:
1) Start the return for your Medium shirt from ORD-7215, and  
2) Walk you through placing the new order for the Large so there’s no guesswork.

I understand that’s the policy you’re quoting, but that’s really not what I’m asking for—I don’t want a return, store credit, or any kind of refund path at all, just a direct exchange of the Premium Cotton Shirt in Medium for the Premium Cotton Shirt in Large.  

Is there any way you can treat this as a same-item size exchange on your side so it stays a simple swap with no credit or refund issued to anyone?

I can’t override that, unfortunately.

Our system literally doesn’t support a “same item, different size” swap without running it through the return/exchange flow, and that flow always treats a same-item request as a return (with either a refund or store credit). There isn’t a hidden option I can use to silently swap the Medium for a Large with no value issued to anyone.

The closest I can get to what you want is:
- Return the Medium shirt as a **gift return** tied to your account, and  
- Apply the value as **store credit only to you**, then immediately use it to order the Large.

If you decide you’re okay with that workaround, I’ll start it for ITEM-10215 and guide you step by step so it feels as much like a simple size swap as possible.

I really appreciate you explaining how your system works, but a **gift return with store credit** still isn’t what I’m asking for—I specifically don’t want this processed as a return, refund, or credit in any form.  

I only want the existing Premium Cotton Shirt in Medium from ORD-7215 exchanged directly for the Premium Cotton Shirt in Large, with no refund or store credit issued at all—could you check whether there’s an internal way (like a same-variant exchange option for my gold account) that can be used instead of the gift-return path?

I checked what I can do on my side, including options tied to your gold status, and there isn’t an internal path that lets me process a same-item, different-size swap without it going through the return/exchange system in some form. The tools I have simply don’t support a “pure swap” that leaves no refund or credit record at all.

I know that’s not the answer you were hoping for, but I don’t have a way to secretly convert your Medium to a Large without creating either a return or store-credit transaction in the system. If you change your mind, I can still set this up as a gift return that issues value only to you and immediately use it to order the Large, or I can walk you through any other option you’d prefer.

Thanks for checking into it so thoroughly—I understand if the tools just don’t allow a pure size swap with no return or credit record.  

In that case I’ll pass for now, since I really don’t want this handled as a return, refund, or store credit in any form, but I appreciate your help explaining the options. [TASK_DONE]
